title: Diagonal Squeeze
setup: King and queen are stacked on the **h-file** — but the mate is hiding off to the side.
hint: Don't deliver check from h7 — that's stalemate. Cross to the long diagonal so {{g7}} stays covered by your king.
explanation:
## The Idea

This position is a classic **stalemate trap** disguised as a mate. The natural move {{Qg6}} or {{Qh7}} would either be stalemate or let the king to g8. The trick is to come from the side.

Play {{Qe8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{g8}} — covered by the queen along the 8th rank
- {{g7}} — controlled by your own king on **h6**
- {{h7}} — also controlled by your king on h6
- The king cannot capture (e8 is far) and nothing can interpose

> **Takeaway:** In the K+Q vs K endgame, beware of stalemate. The queen often delivers mate from a *knight's move away* from the enemy king — never directly adjacent unless your own king defends her.
